explanation: The natural language statement specifies a requirement during the 'Start-up Mode': that default parameters shall be loaded. This is best captured by a global implication, stating that whenever the system is in 'StartUpMode', the 'defaultParamsLoaded' condition must hold true. Therefore, template 30, (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)), is chosen, where bool_exp1 represents 'StartUpMode' and bool_exp3 represents 'defaultParamsLoaded'.

The LTL property states that globally, whenever the system's initialization process begins (represented by the atomic proposition "initStart"), it must be the case that a set of default parameters is loaded in the immediate next state (represented by the atomic proposition "defaultParamsLoaded"). This directly translates the requirement that default values are provided and loaded from a configuration file when the machine is turned on, as its turn-on sequence is typically initiated by the initialization start.

The natural language states that a set of default values shall be provided and loaded when the machine is turned on, specifically within the context of 'Start-up Mode'. This implies that whenever the system is in 'Start-up Mode', the default parameters must be in a loaded state. Therefore, the LTL property is a global implication: if the system is in 'StartUpMode', then 'defaultParamsLoaded' must be true.

This property states that globally, whenever the system/controller initialization starts (initStart), the default parameters are loaded (defaultParamsLoaded) in the very next time step. This accurately captures the requirement that the loading of default parameters is a direct consequence of the machine being turned on, as indicated by the 'initStart' event.

The natural language statement specifies that when the machine is turned on (interpreted as the system initialization starting, represented by 'initStart'), default parameters must be loaded ('defaultParamsLoaded'). This is a guarantee property stating that if 'initStart' occurs, 'defaultParamsLoaded' must eventually become true. Template 39, G (bool_exp1 -> F bool_exp3), precisely captures this requirement, where 'bool_exp1' is the trigger 'initStart' and 'bool_exp3' is the eventual outcome 'defaultParamsLoaded'.

The property states that globally, whenever the system initialization starts (representing the machine being turned on), the default parameters will eventually be loaded. This directly captures the requirement that default values shall be loaded when the machine is turned on.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'Start-up Mode', it must ensure that 'defaultParamsLoaded' becomes true within N_DURATION time units. This directly captures the requirement that a set of default values shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file when the machine is turned on, as this action is integral to the 'Start-up Mode'.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'StartUpMode' (which describes the process of initialization when the machine is turned on), it must eventually reach a state where 'defaultParamsLoaded' is true. This captures the requirement that default parameters shall be loaded from a configuration file when the machine is turned on, implying this action occurs during the Start-up Mode.

The property states that whenever the system enters 'StartUpMode' (i.e., 'StartUpMode' becomes true while it was false in the previous state), then 'defaultParamsLoaded' must be true in the very next state. Additionally, if the system is in 'StartUpMode' at the initial moment (time 0), 'defaultParamsLoaded' must also be true at that same initial moment. This captures the requirement that default parameters are loaded when the machine is turned on and enters the start-up mode.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in Start-up Mode (StartUpMode), a set of default parameters (defaultParamsLoaded) must eventually be loaded within a defined duration (N_DURATION). This captures the requirement that loading default parameters is a necessary part of the "Start-up Mode" procedure, which occurs when the machine is turned on.
